text.title: Attribution Strategy, Configuration, and Incrementality Testing
text.summary: Choosing a unified source of truth, configuring GA4 and platform attribution settings, and running geo-holdout incrementality tests.

rule.statement: Pick one attribution source of truth (usually GA4 data-driven or warehouse on top of GA4 export) and apply it consistently across all budget decisions.
rule.rationale: Switching models mid-flight invalidates historical comparisons and causes confusion during budget reviews. A documented, locked model is more valuable than the theoretically best model.

list.item: Trust GA4 (data-driven): unified view, misses view-through — best for most teams
Warehouse-deduped: most accurate, requires ETL pipeline — best for $10k+/month spend
Trust platform data: detailed but over-counts — use only for within-platform optimization
Incrementality (geo-holdout): true causal lift — use quarterly to validate model
Admin → Attribution settings → Reporting attribution model: Data-driven
Lookback window: 30-90 days (match to sales cycle)

rule.statement: Run a geo-holdout incrementality test at least once per quarter when spending $10k+/month to validate the attribution model.
rule.rationale: Data-driven attribution drifts over time and cannot detect true causal lift. A geo-holdout measures actual revenue lift vs. a control region, providing ground truth that no model can replicate.

list.item: Geo holdout: no ads in test regions, compare conversion lift vs. control
Conversion lift: platform-native measurement of lift over holdout
Ghost ads: control group sees placebo ads, not the tested campaign

rule.statement: Run incrementality tests for at least 4 weeks and do not change budgets or targeting during the test period.
rule.rationale: Short tests produce noise, not signal. Budget changes mid-test contaminate the treatment-control comparison and invalidate results.

antipattern.description: Running an incrementality test for less than 2 weeks or adjusting budgets during the test.
antipattern.reason: Insufficient runtime produces statistically noisy results. Mid-test budget changes alter the treatment condition and make the holdout comparison invalid.

antipattern.description: Trusting platform-reported totals for cross-channel budget decisions.
antipattern.reason: Platform totals are over-counted by 20-40% due to cross-channel overlap. Decisions based on them systematically overspend on channels that appear more efficient than they are.

antipattern.description: Using last-click as the sole attribution model for a multi-touch journey.
antipattern.reason: Last-click assigns zero credit to all upper-funnel touchpoints, leading to systematic under-investment in awareness and consideration channels.
Display and video channels drive brand recall that appears as direct traffic. Excluding view-through undervalues awareness spend.
